memes.description: "Jupiter announced that he intended to appoint a king over the birds, and named a day on which they were to appear before his throne, when he would select the..."

memes.label: "THE TRAVELLER AND HIS DOG"
memes.description: "A Traveller was about to start on a journey, and said to his Dog, who was stretching himself by the door, \"Come, what are you yawning for? Hurry up and get r..."

memes.label: "THE SHIPWRECKED MAN AND THE SEA"
memes.description: "A Shipwrecked Man cast up on the beach fell asleep after his struggle with the waves."

memes.label: "THE WILD BOAR AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "A Wild Boar was engaged in whetting his tusks upon the trunk of a tree in the forest when a Fox came by and, seeing what he was at, said to him, \"Why are you..."

memes.label: "MERCURY AND THE SCULPTOR"
memes.description: "Mercury was very anxious to know in what estimation he was held by mankind; so he disguised himself as a man and walked into a Sculptor's studio, where there..."

memes.label: "THE FAWN AND HIS MOTHER"
memes.description: "A Hind said to her Fawn, who was now well grown and strong, \"My son, Nature has given you a powerful body and a stout pair of horns, and I can't think why yo..."

memes.label: "THE FOX AND THE LION"
memes.description: "A Fox who had never seen a Lion one day met one, and was so terrified at the sight of him that he was ready to die with fear."

memes.label: "THE EAGLE AND HIS CAPTOR"
memes.description: "A Man once caught an Eagle, and after clipping his wings turned him loose among the fowls in his hen-house, where he moped in a corner, looking very dejected..."

memes.label: "THE BLACKSMITH AND HIS DOG"
memes.description: "A Blacksmith had a little Dog, which used to sleep when his master was at work, but was very wide awake indeed when it was time for meals."

memes.label: "THE STAG AT THE POOL"
memes.description: "A thirsty Stag went down to a pool to drink."

memes.label: "THE DOG AND THE SHADOW"
memes.description: "A Dog was crossing a plank bridge over a stream with a piece of meat in his mouth, when he happened to see his own reflection in the water."

memes.label: "MERCURY AND THE TRADESMEN"
memes.description: "When Jupiter was creating man, he told Mercury to make an infusion of lies, and to add a little of it to the other ingredients which went to the making of th..."

memes.label: "THE MICE AND THE WEASELS"
memes.description: "There was war between the Mice and the Weasels, in which the Mice always got the worst of it, numbers of them being killed and eaten by the Weasels."

memes.label: "THE PEACOCK AND JUNO"
memes.description: "The Peacock was greatly discontented because he had not a beautiful voice like the nightingale, and he went and complained to Juno about it."

memes.label: "THE BEAR AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "A Bear was once bragging about his generous feelings, and saying how refined he was compared with other animals."

memes.label: "THE ASS AND THE OLD PEASANT"
memes.description: "An old Peasant was sitting in a meadow watching his Ass, which was grazing close by, when all of a sudden he caught sight of armed men stealthily approaching."

memes.label: "THE OX AND THE FROG"
memes.description: "Two little Frogs were playing about at the edge of a pool when an Ox came down to the water to drink, and by accident trod on one of them and crushed the lif..."

memes.label: "THE MAN AND THE IMAGE"
memes.description: "A poor Man had a wooden Image of a god, to which he used to pray daily for riches."

memes.label: "HERCULES AND THE WAGGONER"
memes.description: "A Waggoner was driving his team along a muddy lane with a full load behind them, when the wheels of his waggon sank so deep in the mire that no efforts of hi..."

memes.label: "THE POMEGRANATE, THE APPLE-TREE, AND THE BRAMBLE"
memes.description: "A Pomegranate and an Apple-tree were disputing about the quality of their fruits, and each claimed that its own was the better of the two."

memes.label: "THE LION, THE BEAR, AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "A Lion and a Bear were fighting for possession of a kid, which they had both seized at the same moment."

memes.label: "THE BLACKAMOOR"
memes.description: "A Man once bought an Ethiopian slave, who had a black skin like all Ethiopians; but his new master thought his colour was due to his late owner's having negl..."

memes.label: "THE TWO SOLDIERS AND THE ROBBER"
memes.description: "Two Soldiers travelling together were set upon by a Robber."

memes.label: "THE LION AND THE WILD ASS"
memes.description: "A Lion and a Wild Ass went out hunting together: the latter was to run down the prey by his superior speed, and the former would then come up and despatch it."

memes.label: "THE MAN AND THE SATYR"
memes.description: "A Man and a Satyr became friends, and determined to live together."

memes.label: "THE IMAGE-SELLER"
memes.description: "A certain man made a wooden Image of Mercury, and exposed it for sale in the market."

memes.label: "THE EAGLE AND THE ARROW"
memes.description: "An Eagle sat perched on a lofty rock, keeping a sharp look-out for prey."

memes.label: "THE RICH MAN AND THE TANNER"
memes.description: "A Rich Man took up his residence next door to a Tanner, and found the smell of the tan-yard so extremely unpleasant that he told him he must go."

memes.label: "THE WOLF, THE MOTHER, AND HER CHILD"
memes.description: "A hungry Wolf was prowling about in search of food."

memes.label: "THE OLD WOMAN AND THE WINE-JAR"
memes.description: "An old Woman picked up an empty Wine-jar which had once contained a rare and costly wine, and which still retained some traces of its exquisite bouquet."

memes.label: "THE LIONESS AND THE VIXEN"
memes.description: "A Lioness and a Vixen were talking together about their young, as mothers will, and saying how healthy and well-grown they were, and what beautiful coats the..."

memes.label: "THE VIPER AND THE FILE"
memes.description: "A Viper entered a carpenter's shop, and went from one to another of the tools, begging for something to eat."

memes.label: "THE CAT AND THE COCK"
memes.description: "A Cat pounced on a Cock, and cast about for some good excuse for making a meal off him, for Cats don't as a rule eat Cocks, and she knew she ought not to."

memes.label: "THE HARE AND THE TORTOISE"
memes.description: "A Hare was one day making fun of a Tortoise for being so slow upon his feet."

memes.label: "THE SOLDIER AND HIS HORSE"
memes.description: "A Soldier gave his Horse a plentiful supply of oats in time of war, and tended him with the utmost care, for he wished him to be strong to endure the hardshi..."

memes.label: "THE OXEN AND THE BUTCHERS"
memes.description: "Once upon a time the Oxen determined to be revenged upon the Butchers for the havoc they wrought in their ranks, and plotted to put them to death on a given ..."

memes.label: "THE WOLF AND THE LION"
memes.description: "A wolf stole a lamb from the flock, and was carrying it off to devour it at his leisure when he met a Lion, who took his prey away from him and walked off wi..."

memes.label: "THE SHEEP, THE WOLF, AND THE STAG"
memes.description: "A Stag once asked a Sheep to lend him a measure of wheat, saying that his friend the Wolf would be his surety."

memes.label: "THE LION AND THE THREE BULLS"
memes.description: "Three Bulls were grazing in a meadow, and were watched by a Lion, who longed to capture and devour them, but who felt that he was no match for the three so l..."

memes.label: "THE HORSE AND HIS RIDER"
memes.description: "A Young Man, who fancied himself something of a horseman, mounted a Horse which had not been properly broken in, and was exceedingly difficult to control."

memes.label: "THE GOAT AND THE VINE"
memes.description: "A Goat was straying in a vineyard, and began to browse on the tender shoots of a Vine which bore several fine bunches of grapes."

memes.label: "THE TWO POTS"
memes.description: "Two Pots, one of earthenware and the other of brass, were carried away down a river in flood."

memes.label: "THE OLD HOUND"
memes.description: "A Hound who had served his master well for years, and had run down many a quarry in his time, began to lose his strength and speed owing to age."

memes.label: "THE CLOWN AND THE COUNTRYMAN"
memes.description: "A Nobleman announced his intention of giving a public entertainment in the theatre, and offered splendid prizes to all who had any novelty to exhibit at the ..."

memes.label: "THE LARK AND THE FARMER"
memes.description: "A Lark nested in a field of corn, and was rearing her brood under cover of the ripening grain."

memes.label: "THE LION AND THE ASS"
memes.description: "A Lion and an Ass set up as partners and went a-hunting together."

memes.label: "THE PROPHET"
memes.description: "A Prophet sat in the market-place and told the fortunes of all who cared to engage his services."

memes.label: "THE HOUND AND THE HARE"
memes.description: "A young Hound started a Hare, and, when he caught her up, would at one moment snap at her with his teeth as though he were about to kill her, while at anothe..."

memes.label: "THE LION, THE MOUSE, AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "A Lion was lying asleep at the mouth of his den when a Mouse ran over his back and tickled him so that he woke up with a start and began looking about everyw..."

memes.label: "THE TRUMPETER TAKEN PRISONER"
memes.description: "A Trumpeter marched into battle in the van of the army and put courage into his comrades by his warlike tunes."

memes.label: "THE WOLF AND THE CRANE"
memes.description: "A Wolf once got a bone stuck in his throat."

memes.label: "THE EAGLE, THE CAT, AND THE WILD SOW"
memes.description: "An Eagle built her nest at the top of a high tree; a Cat with her family occupied a hollow in the trunk half-way down; and a Wild Sow and her young took up t..."

memes.label: "THE WOLF AND THE SHEEP"
memes.description: "A Wolf was worried and badly bitten by dogs, and lay a long time for dead."

memes.label: "THE TUNNY-FISH AND THE DOLPHIN"
memes.description: "A Tunny-fish was chased by a Dolphin and splashed through the water at a great rate, but the Dolphin gradually gained upon him, and was just about to seize h..."

memes.label: "THE THREE TRADESMEN"
memes.description: "The citizens of a certain city were debating about the best material to use in the fortifications which were about to be erected for the greater security of ..."

memes.label: "THE MOUSE AND THE BULL"
memes.description: "A Bull gave chase to a Mouse which had bitten him in the nose: but the Mouse was too quick for him and slipped into a hole in a wall."

memes.label: "THE HARE AND THE HOUND"
memes.description: "A Hound started a Hare from her form, and pursued her for some distance; but as she gradually gained upon him, he gave up the chase."

memes.label: "THE TOWN MOUSE AND THE COUNTRY MOUSE"
memes.description: "A Town Mouse and a Country Mouse were acquaintances, and the Country Mouse one day invited his friend to come and see him at his home in the fields."

memes.label: "THE LION AND THE BULL"
memes.description: "A Lion saw a fine fat Bull pasturing among a herd of cattle and cast about for some means of getting him into his clutches; so he sent him word that he was s..."

memes.label: "THE WOLF, THE FOX, AND THE APE"
memes.description: "A Wolf charged a Fox with theft, which he denied, and the case was brought before an Ape to be tried."

memes.label: "THE EAGLE AND THE COCKS"
memes.description: "There were two Cocks in the same farmyard, and they fought to decide who should be master."

memes.label: "THE ESCAPED JACKDAW"
memes.description: "A Man caught a Jackdaw and tied a piece of string to one of its legs, and then gave it to his children for a pet."

memes.label: "THE FARMER AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "A Farmer was greatly annoyed by a Fox, which came prowling about his yard at night and carried off his fowls."

memes.label: "VENUS AND THE CAT"
memes.description: "A Cat fell in love with a handsome young man, and begged the goddess Venus to change her into a woman."

memes.label: "THE CROW AND THE SWAN"
memes.description: "A Crow was filled with envy on seeing the beautiful white plumage of a Swan, and thought it was due to the water in which the Swan constantly bathed and swam."

memes.label: "THE STAG WITH ONE EYE"
memes.description: "A Stag, blind of one eye, was grazing close to the sea-shore and kept his sound eye turned towards the land, so as to be able to perceive the approach of the..."

memes.label: "THE FLY AND THE DRAUGHT-MULE"
memes.description: "A Fly sat on one of the shafts of a cart and said to the Mule who was pulling it, \"How slow you are! Do mend your pace, or I shall have to use my sting as a ..."

memes.label: "THE COCK AND THE JEWEL"
memes.description: "A Cock, scratching the ground for something to eat, turned up a Jewel that had by chance been dropped there."

memes.label: "THE WOLF AND THE SHEPHERD"
memes.description: "A Wolf hung about near a flock of sheep for a long time, but made no attempt to molest them."

memes.label: "THE FARMER AND THE STORK"
memes.description: "A Farmer set some traps in a field which he had lately sown with corn, in order to catch the cranes which came to pick up the seed."

memes.label: "THE CHARGER AND THE MILLER"
memes.description: "A Horse, who had been used to carry his rider into battle, felt himself growing old and chose to work in a mill instead."

memes.label: "THE GRASSHOPPER AND THE OWL"
memes.description: "An Owl, who lived in a hollow tree, was in the habit of feeding by night and sleeping by day; but her slumbers were greatly disturbed by the chirping of a Gr..."

memes.label: "THE GRASSHOPPER AND THE ANTS"
memes.description: "One fine day in winter some Ants were busy drying their store of corn, which had got rather damp during a long spell of rain."

memes.label: "THE FARMER AND THE VIPER"
memes.description: "One winter a Farmer found a Viper frozen and numb with cold, and out of pity picked it up and placed it in his bosom."

memes.label: "THE TWO FROGS"
memes.description: "Two Frogs were neighbours."

memes.label: "THE COBBLER TURNED DOCTOR"
memes.description: "A very unskilful Cobbler, finding himself unable to make a living at his trade, gave up mending boots and took to doctoring instead."

memes.label: "THE ASS, THE COCK, AND THE LION"
memes.description: "An Ass and a Cock were in a cattle-pen together."

memes.label: "THE BELLY AND THE MEMBERS"
memes.description: "The Members of the Body once rebelled against the Belly."

memes.label: "THE BALD MAN AND THE FLY"
memes.description: "A Fly settled on the head of a Bald Man and bit him."

memes.label: "THE ASS AND THE WOLF"
memes.description: "An Ass was feeding in a meadow, and, catching sight of his enemy the Wolf in the distance, pretended to be very lame and hobbled painfully along."

memes.label: "THE MONKEY AND THE CAMEL"
memes.description: "At a gathering of all the beasts the Monkey gave an exhibition of dancing and entertained the company vastly."

memes.label: "THE SICK MAN AND THE DOCTOR"
memes.description: "A Sick Man received a visit from his Doctor, who asked him how he was."

memes.label: "THE TRAVELLERS AND THE PLANE-TREE"
memes.description: "Two Travellers were walking along a bare and dusty road in the heat of a summer's day."

memes.label: "THE FLEA AND THE OX"
memes.description: "A Flea once said to an Ox, \"How comes it that a big strong fellow like you is content to serve mankind, and do all their hard work for them, while I, who am ..."

memes.label: "THE BIRDS, THE BEASTS, AND THE BAT"
memes.description: "The Birds were at war with the Beasts, and many battles were fought with varying success on either side."

memes.label: "THE MAN AND HIS TWO SWEETHEARTS"
memes.description: "A Man of middle age, whose hair was turning grey, had two Sweethearts, an old woman and a young one."

memes.label: "THE EAGLE, THE JACKDAW, AND THE SHEPHERD"
memes.description: "One day a Jackdaw saw an Eagle swoop down on a lamb and carry it off in its talons."

memes.label: "THE WOLF AND THE BOY"
memes.description: "A Wolf, who had just enjoyed a good meal and was in a playful mood, caught sight of a Boy lying flat upon the ground, and, realising that he was trying to hi..."

memes.label: "THE MILLER, HIS SON, AND THEIR ASS"
memes.description: "A Miller, accompanied by his young Son, was driving his Ass to market in hopes of finding a purchaser for him."

memes.label: "THE STAG AND THE VINE"
memes.description: "A Stag, pursued by the huntsmen, concealed himself under cover of a thick Vine."

memes.label: "THE LAMB CHASED BY A WOLF"
memes.description: "A Wolf was chasing a Lamb, which took refuge in a temple."

memes.label: "THE ARCHER AND THE LION"
memes.description: "An Archer went up into the hills to get some sport with his bow, and all the animals fled at the sight of him with the exception of the Lion, who stayed behi..."

memes.label: "THE WOLF AND THE GOAT"
memes.description: "A Wolf caught sight of a Goat browsing above him on the scanty herbage that grew on the top of a steep rock; and being unable to get at her, tried to induce ..."

memes.label: "THE SICK STAG"
memes.description: "A Stag fell sick and lay in a clearing in the forest, too weak to move from the spot."

memes.label: "THE ASS AND THE MULE"
memes.description: "A certain man who had an Ass and a Mule loaded them both up one day and set out upon a journey."

memes.label: "BROTHER AND SISTER"
memes.description: "A certain man had two children, a boy and a girl: and the boy was as good-looking as the girl was plain."

memes.label: "THE HEIFER AND THE OX"
memes.description: "A Heifer went up to an Ox, who was straining hard at the plough, and sympathised with him in a rather patronising sort of way on the necessity of his having ..."

memes.label: "THE KINGDOM OF THE LION"
memes.description: "When the Lion reigned over the beasts of the earth he was never cruel or tyrannical, but as gentle and just as a King ought to be."

memes.label: "THE ASS AND HIS DRIVER"
memes.description: "An Ass was being driven down a mountain road, and after jogging along for a while sensibly enough he suddenly quitted the track and rushed to the edge of a p..."

memes.label: "THE LION AND THE HARE"
memes.description: "A Lion found a Hare sleeping in her form, and was just going to devour her when he caught sight of a passing stag."

memes.label: "THE WOLVES AND THE DOGS"
memes.description: "Once upon a time the Wolves said to the Dogs, \"Why should we continue to be enemies any longer? You are very like us in most ways: the main difference betwee..."

memes.label: "THE BULL AND THE CALF"
memes.description: "A full-grown Bull was struggling to force his huge bulk through the narrow entrance to a cow-house where his stall was, when a young Calf came up and said to..."

memes.label: "THE TREES AND THE AXE"
memes.description: "A Woodman went into the forest and begged of the Trees the favour of a handle for his Axe."

memes.label: "THE ASTRONOMER"
memes.description: "There was once an Astronomer whose habit it was to go out at night and observe the stars."

memes.label: "THE LABOURER AND THE SNAKE"
memes.description: "A Labourer's little son was bitten by a Snake and died of the wound."

memes.label: "THE CAGE-BIRD AND THE BAT"
memes.description: "A Singing-bird was confined in a cage which hung outside a window, and had a way of singing at night when all other birds were asleep."

memes.label: "THE ASS AND HIS PURCHASER"
memes.description: "A Man who wanted to buy an Ass went to market, and, coming across a likely-looking beast, arranged with the owner that he should be allowed to take him home ..."

memes.label: "THE KID AND THE WOLF"
memes.description: "A Kid strayed from the flock and was chased by a Wolf."

memes.label: "THE DEBTOR AND HIS SOW"
memes.description: "A Man of Athens fell into debt and was pressed for the money by his creditor; but he had no means of paying at the time, so he begged for delay."

memes.label: "THE BALD HUNTSMAN"
memes.description: "A Man who had lost all his hair took to wearing a wig, and one day he went out hunting."

memes.label: "THE HERDSMAN AND THE LOST BULL"
memes.description: "A Herdsman was tending his cattle when he missed a young Bull, one of the finest of the herd."

memes.label: "THE MULE"
memes.description: "One morning a Mule, who had too much to eat and too little to do, began to think himself a very fine fellow indeed, and frisked about saying, \"My father was ..."

memes.label: "THE HOUND AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "A Hound, roaming in the forest, spied a lion, and being well used to lesser game, gave chase, thinking he would make a fine quarry."

memes.label: "THE FATHER AND HIS DAUGHTERS"
memes.description: "A Man had two Daughters, one of whom he gave in marriage to a gardener, and the other to a potter."

memes.label: "THE THIEF AND THE INNKEEPER"
memes.description: "A Thief hired a room at an inn, and stayed there some days on the look-out for something to steal."

memes.label: "THE PACK-ASS AND THE WILD ASS"
memes.description: "A Wild Ass, who was wandering idly about, one day came upon a Pack-Ass lying at full length in a sunny spot and thoroughly enjoying himself."

memes.label: "THE ASS AND HIS MASTERS"
memes.description: "A Gardener had an Ass which had a very hard time of it, what with scanty food, heavy loads, and constant beating."

memes.label: "THE PACK-ASS, THE WILD ASS, AND THE LION"
memes.description: "A Wild Ass saw a Pack-Ass jogging along under a heavy load, and taunted him with the condition of slavery in which he lived, in these words: \"What a vile lot..."

memes.label: "THE ANT"
memes.description: "Ants were once men and made their living by tilling the soil."

memes.label: "THE FROGS AND THE WELL"
memes.description: "Two Frogs lived together in a marsh."

memes.label: "THE CRAB AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "A Crab once left the sea-shore and went and settled in a meadow some way inland, which looked very nice and green and seemed likely to be a good place to fee..."

memes.label: "THE FOX AND THE GRASSHOPPER"
memes.description: "A Grasshopper sat chirping in the branches of a tree."

memes.label: "THE FARMER, HIS BOY, AND THE ROOKS"
memes.description: "A Farmer had just sown a field of wheat, and was keeping a careful watch over it, for numbers of Rooks and starlings kept continually settling on it and eati..."

memes.label: "THE ASS AND THE DOG"
memes.description: "An Ass and a Dog were on their travels together, and, as they went along, they found a sealed packet lying on the ground."

memes.label: "THE ASS CARRYING THE IMAGE"
memes.description: "A certain man put an Image on the back of his Ass to take it to one of the temples of the town."

memes.label: "THE ATHENIAN AND THE THEBAN"
memes.description: "An Athenian and a Theban were on the road together, and passed the time in conversation, as is the way of travellers."

memes.label: "THE GOATHERD AND THE GOAT"
memes.description: "A Goatherd was one day gathering his flock to return to the fold, when one of his goats strayed and refused to join the rest."

memes.label: "THE SHEEP AND THE DOG"
memes.description: "Once upon a time the Sheep complained to the shepherd about the difference in his treatment of themselves and his Dog."

memes.label: "THE SHEPHERD AND THE WOLF"
memes.description: "A Shepherd found a Wolf's Cub straying in the pastures, and took him home and reared him along with his dogs."

memes.label: "THE LION, JUPITER, AND THE ELEPHANT"
memes.description: "The Lion, for all his size and strength, and his sharp teeth and claws, is a coward in one thing: he can't bear the sound of a cock crowing, and runs away wh..."

memes.label: "THE PIG AND THE SHEEP"
memes.description: "A Pig found his way into a meadow where a flock of Sheep were grazing."

memes.label: "THE GARDENER AND HIS DOG"
memes.description: "A Gardner's Dog fell into a deep well, from which his master used to draw water for the plants in his garden with a rope and a bucket."

memes.label: "THE RIVERS AND THE SEA"
memes.description: "Once upon a time all the Rivers combined to protest against the action of the Sea in making their waters salt."

memes.label: "THE LION IN LOVE"
memes.description: "A Lion fell deeply in love with the daughter of a cottager and wanted to marry her; but her father was unwilling to give her to so fearsome a husband, and ye..."

memes.label: "THE BEE-KEEPER"
memes.description: "A Thief found his way into an apiary when the Bee-keeper was away, and stole all the honey."

memes.label: "THE WOLF AND THE HORSE"
memes.description: "A Wolf on his rambles came to a field of oats, but, not being able to eat them, he was passing on his way when a Horse came along."

memes.label: "THE BAT, THE BRAMBLE, AND THE SEAGULL"
memes.description: "A Bat, a Bramble, and a Seagull went into partnership and determined to go on a trading voyage together."

memes.label: "THE DOG AND THE WOLF"
memes.description: "A Dog was lying in the sun before a farmyard gate when a Wolf pounced upon him and was just going to eat him up; but he begged for his life and said, \"You se..."

memes.label: "THE WASP AND THE SNAKE"
memes.description: "A Wasp settled on the head of a Snake, and not only stung him several times, but clung obstinately to the head of his victim."

memes.label: "THE EAGLE AND THE BEETLE"
memes.description: "An Eagle was chasing a hare, which was running for dear life and was at her wits' end to know where to turn for help."

memes.label: "THE FOWLER AND THE LARK"
memes.description: "A Fowler was setting his nets for little birds when a Lark came up to him and asked him what he was doing."

memes.label: "THE FISHERMAN PIPING"
memes.description: "A Fisherman who could play the flute went down one day to the sea-shore with his nets and his flute; and, taking his stand on a projecting rock, began to pla..."

memes.label: "THE WEASEL AND THE MAN"
memes.description: "A Man once caught a Weasel, which was always sneaking about the house, and was just going to drown it in a tub of water, when it begged hard for its life, an..."

memes.label: "THE PLOUGHMAN, THE ASS, AND THE OX"
memes.description: "A Ploughman yoked his Ox and his Ass together, and set to work to plough his field."

memes.label: "DEMADES AND HIS FABLE"
memes.description: "Demades the orator was once speaking in the Assembly at Athens; but the people were very inattentive to what he was saying, so he stopped and said, \"Gentleme..."

memes.label: "THE MONKEY AND THE DOLPHIN"
memes.description: "When people go on a voyage they often take with them lap-dogs or monkeys as pets to wile away the time."

memes.label: "THE CROW AND THE SNAKE"
memes.description: "A hungry Crow spied a Snake lying asleep in a sunny spot, and, picking it up in his claws, he was carrying it off to a place where he could make a meal of it..."

memes.label: "THE DOGS AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "Some Dogs once found a lion's skin, and were worrying it with their teeth."

memes.label: "THE NIGHTINGALE AND THE HAWK"
memes.description: "A Nightingale was sitting on a bough of an oak and singing, as her custom was."

memes.label: "THE ROSE AND THE AMARANTH"
memes.description: "A Rose and an Amaranth blossomed side by side in a garden, and the Amaranth said to her neighbour, \"How I envy you your beauty and your sweet scent! No wonde..."

memes.label: "THE MAN, THE HORSE, THE OX, AND THE DOG"
memes.description: "One winter's day, during a severe storm, a Horse, an Ox, and a Dog came and begged for shelter in the house of a Man."

memes.label: "THE WOLVES, THE SHEEP, AND THE RAM"
memes.description: "The Wolves sent a deputation to the Sheep with proposals for a lasting peace between them, on condition of their giving up the sheep-dogs to instant death."

memes.label: "THE SWAN"
memes.description: "The Swan is said to sing but once in its life—when it knows that it is about to die."

memes.label: "THE SNAKE AND JUPITER"
memes.description: "A Snake suffered a good deal from being constantly trodden upon by man and beast, owing partly to the length of his body and partly to his being unable to ra..."

memes.label: "THE WOLF AND HIS SHADOW"
memes.description: "A Wolf, who was roaming about on the plain when the sun was getting low in the sky, was much impressed by the size of his shadow, and said to himself, \"I had..."

memes.label: "THE PLOUGHMAN AND THE WOLF"
memes.description: "A Ploughman loosed his oxen from the plough, and led them away to the water to drink."

memes.label: "MERCURY AND THE MAN BITTEN BY AN ANT"
memes.description: "A Man once saw a ship go down with all its crew, and commented severely on the injustice of the gods."

memes.label: "THE WILY LION"
memes.description: "A Lion watched a fat Bull feeding in a meadow, and his mouth watered when he thought of the royal feast he would make, but he did not dare to attack him, for..."

memes.label: "THE PARROT AND THE CAT"
memes.description: "A Man once bought a Parrot and gave it the run of his house."

memes.label: "THE STAG AND THE LION"
memes.description: "A Stag was chased by the hounds, and took refuge in a cave, where he hoped to be safe from his pursuers."

memes.label: "THE IMPOSTOR"
memes.description: "A certain man fell ill, and, being in a very bad way, he made a vow that he would sacrifice a hundred oxen to the gods if they would grant him a return to he..."

memes.label: "THE DOGS AND THE HIDES"
memes.description: "Once upon a time a number of Dogs, who were famished with hunger, saw some Hides steeping in a river, but couldn't get at them because the water was too deep."

memes.label: "THE LION, THE FOX, AND THE ASS"
memes.description: "A Lion, a Fox, and an Ass went out hunting together."

memes.label: "THE FOWLER, THE PARTRIDGE, AND THE COCK"
memes.description: "One day, as a Fowler was sitting down to a scanty supper of herbs and bread, a friend dropped in unexpectedly."

memes.label: "THE GNAT AND THE LION"
memes.description: "A Gnat once went up to a Lion and said, \"I am not in the least afraid of you: I don't even allow that you are a match for me in strength."

memes.label: "THE FARMER AND HIS DOGS"
memes.description: "A Farmer was snowed up in his farmstead by a severe storm, and was unable to go out and procure provisions for himself and his family."

memes.label: "THE EAGLE AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "An Eagle and a Fox became great friends and determined to live near one another: they thought that the more they saw of each other the better friends they wo..."

memes.label: "THE BUTCHER AND HIS CUSTOMERS"
memes.description: "Two Men were buying meat at a Butcher's stall in the market-place, and, while the Butcher's back was turned for a moment, one of them snatched up a joint and..."

memes.label: "HERCULES AND MINERVA"
memes.description: "Hercules was once travelling along a narrow road when he saw lying on the ground in front of him what appeared to be an apple, and as he passed he stamped up..."

memes.label: "THE FOX WHO SERVED A LION"
memes.description: "A Lion had a Fox to attend on him, and whenever they went hunting the Fox found the prey and the Lion fell upon it and killed it, and then they divided it be..."

memes.label: "THE QUACK DOCTOR"
memes.description: "A certain man fell sick and took to his bed."

memes.label: "THE LION, THE WOLF, AND THE FOX"
memes.description: "A Lion, infirm with age, lay sick in his den, and all the beasts of the forest came to inquire after his health with the exception of the Fox."

memes.label: "HERCULES AND PLUTUS"
memes.description: "When Hercules was received among the gods and was entertained at a banquet by Jupiter, he responded courteously to the greetings of all with the exception of..."

memes.label: "THE FOX AND THE LEOPARD"
memes.description: "A Fox and a Leopard were disputing about their looks, and each claimed to be the more handsome of the two."

memes.label: "THE FOX AND THE HEDGEHOG"
memes.description: "A Fox, in swimming across a rapid river, was swept away by the current and carried a long way downstream in spite of his struggles, until at last, bruised an..."

memes.label: "THE CROW AND THE RAVEN"
memes.description: "A Crow became very jealous of a Raven, because the latter was regarded by men as a bird of omen which foretold the future, and was accordingly held in great ..."

memes.label: "THE WITCH"
memes.description: "A Witch professed to be able to avert the anger of the gods by means of charms, of which she alone possessed the secret; and she drove a brisk trade, and mad..."

memes.label: "THE OLD MAN AND DEATH"
memes.description: "An Old Man cut himself a bundle of faggots in a wood and started to carry them home."

memes.label: "THE MISER"
memes.description: "A Miser sold everything he had, and melted down his hoard of gold into a single lump, which he buried secretly in a field."

memes.label: "THE FOXES AND THE RIVER"
memes.description: "A number of Foxes assembled on the bank of a river and wanted to drink; but the current was so strong and the water looked so deep and dangerous that they di..."

memes.label: "THE HORSE AND THE STAG"
memes.description: "There was once a Horse who used to graze in a meadow which he had all to himself."

memes.label: "THE FOX AND THE BRAMBLE"
memes.description: "In making his way through a hedge a Fox missed his footing and caught at a Bramble to save himself from falling."

memes.label: "THE FOX AND THE SNAKE"
memes.description: "A Snake, in crossing a river, was carried away by the current, but managed to wriggle on to a bundle of thorns which was floating by, and was thus carried at..."

memes.label: "THE LION, THE FOX, AND THE STAG"
memes.description: "A Lion lay sick in his den, unable to provide himself with food."

memes.label: "THE MAN WHO LOST HIS SPADE"
memes.description: "A Man was engaged in digging over his vineyard, and one day on coming to work he missed his Spade."

memes.label: "THE PARTRIDGE AND THE FOWLER"
memes.description: "A Fowler caught a Partridge in his nets, and was just about to wring its neck when it made a piteous appeal to him to spare its life and said, \"Do not kill m..."

memes.label: "THE RUNAWAY SLAVE"
memes.description: "A Slave, being discontented with his lot, ran away from his master."

memes.label: "THE HUNTER AND THE WOODMAN"
memes.description: "A Hunter was searching in the forest for the tracks of a lion, and, catching sight presently of a Woodman engaged in felling a tree, he went up to him and as..."

memes.label: "THE SERPENT AND THE EAGLE"
memes.description: "An Eagle swooped down upon a Serpent and seized it in his talons with the intention of carrying it off and devouring it."

memes.label: "THE ROGUE AND THE ORACLE"
memes.description: "A Rogue laid a wager that he would prove the Oracle at Delphi to be untrustworthy by procuring from it a false reply to an inquiry by himself."

memes.label: "THE HORSE AND THE ASS"
memes.description: "A Horse, proud of his fine harness, met an Ass on the high-road."

memes.label: "THE DOG CHASING A WOLF"
memes.description: "A Dog was chasing a Wolf, and as he ran he thought what a fine fellow he was, and what strong legs he had, and how quickly they covered the ground."

memes.label: "GRIEF AND HIS DUE"
memes.description: "When Jupiter was assigning the various gods their privileges, it so happened that Grief was not present with the rest: but when all had received their share,..."

memes.label: "THE HAWK, THE KITE, AND THE PIGEONS"
memes.description: "The Pigeons in a certain dovecote were persecuted by a Kite, who every now and then swooped down and carried off one of their number."

memes.label: "THE WOMAN AND THE FARMER"
memes.description: "A Woman, who had lately lost her husband, used to go every day to his grave and lament her loss."

memes.label: "PROMETHEUS AND THE MAKING OF MAN"
memes.description: "At the bidding of Jupiter, Prometheus set about the creation of Man and the other animals."

memes.label: "THE SWALLOW AND THE CROW"
memes.description: "A Swallow was once boasting to a Crow about her birth."

memes.label: "THE HUNTER AND THE HORSEMAN"
memes.description: "A Hunter went out after game, and succeeded in catching a hare, which he was carrying home with him when he met a man on horseback, who said to him, \"You hav..."

memes.label: "THE GOATHERD AND THE WILD GOATS"
memes.description: "A Goatherd was tending his goats out at pasture when he saw a number of Wild Goats approach and mingle with his flock."

memes.label: "THE NIGHTINGALE AND THE SWALLOW"
memes.description: "A Swallow, conversing with a Nightingale, advised her to quit the leafy coverts where she made her home, and to come and live with men, like herself, and nes..."

memes.label: "THE TRAVELLER AND FORTUNE"
memes.description: "A Traveller, exhausted with fatigue after a long journey, sank down at the very brink of a deep well and presently fell asleep."

memes.label: False Confidence Often Leads to Disaster
memes.description: An Ass, emboldened by a Lion's fear of a Cock's crowing, chases the Lion — only to be eaten once they are beyond earshot of the Cock. The moral diagnoses the danger of borrowed courage: confidence that rests on another's strength, not one's own, collapses the moment the borrowed prop is removed.

memes.label: False Faith Cannot Escape Divine Punishment
memes.description: "An Eagle and a Fox swear friendship, but the Eagle devours the Fox's cubs the moment the Fox leaves to forage. The Fox curses her betrayer from the ground — and divine fire soon brings the Eagle's own fledglings down to the Fox's jaws."
aliases: false faith may escape human punishment but cannot escape the divine

memes.label: Fate
memes.description: The inexorable outcome that awaits each character in Aesop's Fables — portrayed not as blind chance but as the mechanical result of one's choices, distributed by the gods and sometimes recognized too late by its victims.

memes.label: Fear
memes.description: In Aesop's Fables, fear is the great revealer — it strips away pretense, drives the hunted into the hunter's jaws, and exposes character more reliably than any profession of courage.

memes.label: Flattery
memes.description: Praise given for the purpose of manipulation rather than honest admiration — Aesop's Fables treat flattery as the predator's favorite tool, exposing how it exploits the victim's vanity to lower defenses before the strike.

memes.label: Folly
memes.description: The failure to exercise sound judgment — in Aesop's Fables, folly is the root cause of most misfortune, depicted not as a fixed trait but as a correctable failure of reasoning.

memes.label: Foolishness
memes.description: A recurring theme in Aesop's Fables where characters act against their own interests through poor judgment, credulity, or short-sightedness, invariably leading to their downfall.

memes.label: Fortune
memes.description: The unpredictable distribution of good and ill in life — Aesop's Fables portray fortune as fickle, unearned, and a poor guide to character, rewarding prudence over reliance on luck.

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: The state of being free from constraint or captivity — Aesop's Fables weigh the value of freedom against the costs of safety, showing that freedom without prudence is perilous and that true freedom requires wisdom.

relations.label: better servitude with safety than freedom with danger

memes.label: Friends' Quarrels Are Opportunities for Foes
memes.description: "A Lion watches three Bulls graze together, knowing he cannot defeat them united. He sows jealousies among them until each feeds apart — then kills them one by one. Divided friends invite the destruction that united strength would prevent."
aliases: quarrels of friends are opportunities of foes

memes.label: Friendship
memes.description: In Aesop's Fables, friendship is tested by adversity, undermined by ill-matched natures, and celebrated as an ideal of just governance — but never taken for granted.

memes.label: The Frog
memes.description: The amphibian of Aesop's Fables — vulnerable to both boys and predators, prone to foolish ambition, and the creature whose complaint against the Sun and demand for a King define the fable tradition's sharpest political allegories.

memes.label: Give Assistance, Not Advice
memes.description: "A drowning Boy cries for help; a passer-by stops to scold him for carelessness instead of pulling him from the water — prompting the Boy's plea to help first and deliver the lecture afterward."
aliases: give assistance not advice in a crisis

memes.label: The Grasshopper
memes.description: The singing insect of Aesop's Fables — whose devotion to song and present pleasure makes him the definitive symbol of improvidence, flattered by the Owl and rebuked by the Ant in two of the collection's most enduring moral contrasts.

memes.label: Gratitude
memes.description: The recognition and appreciation of benefits received, treated in Aesop's Fables as a moral obligation whose violation brings inevitable consequences.

memes.label: Greatness Carries Its Own Penalties
memes.description: The Aesopic warning that the trappings of greatness — rank, status, distinction — become liabilities in crisis: the Mice generals' plumed helmets prevent their escape while ordinary soldiers flee to safety.

memes.label: Greed
memes.description: An excessive desire for more than one needs or deserves, portrayed across numerous Aesop's fables as a destructive impulse that leads to loss rather than gain.

memes.label: Grief
memes.description: "A profound sorrow caused by loss — in Aesop's Fables, grief is a natural but dangerous emotion: it must be given its due, but prolonged indulgence invites fresh suffering and blinds the grieving to new possibilities."

memes.label: Guilt
memes.description: The state of having committed a wrong — Aesop's Fables depict guilt as something that can be concealed from men but not from the gods, and show how the guilty often convict themselves through their own actions.

memes.label: Habits Change, Nature Stays
memes.description: "A Crow envies a Swan's white plumage and moves from the altars to the streams, bathing constantly to whiten its feathers — but remains black and starves, learning that external routines cannot alter what you fundamentally are."
aliases: you may change your habits but not your nature

memes.label: Harmony
memes.description: The state of peaceful coexistence among parties with divergent interests — in Aesop's Fables, harmony is presented as both a practical necessity for survival and a fragile ideal that requires constant maintenance against the forces of discord.

memes.label: Heaven Helps Those Who Help Themselves
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that divine aid is contingent on human effort — Hercules refuses to help the Waggoner until he first puts his own shoulder to the wheel.
aliases: god helps those who help themselves
put your shoulder to the wheel

memes.label: Hercules
memes.description: Hercules is the legendary Greek hero who appears in Aesop's Fables as a symbol of strength, divine intervention, and the proper relationship between mortals and the gods.

memes.label: Hit Back at the Right Man
memes.description: The Aesopic warning to verify your target before retaliating: the Bee-keeper's anger at the bees misses the real thief, and innocent parties suffer when retribution is misdirected.

memes.label: Honesty
memes.description: The virtue of truthfulness and fair dealing — Aesop's Fables reward honesty with tangible gain and punish dishonesty with loss, treating the honest character as the foundation of trust.

memes.label: Honesty Is the Best Policy
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that truthfulness brings its own reward — illustrated when Mercury rewards an honest Woodman who admits his lost axe is ordinary, while punishing his dishonest companion who claims the golden one.

memes.label: The Hound
memes.description: The hunting dog of Aesop's Fables — a creature of pursuit and instinct, representing the single-minded chase, the courage required to face danger, and the limits of physical disability.

memes.label: A Hypocrite Deceives No One but Himself
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that hypocrisy is primarily self-directed — a Bear who brags about his refined, generous nature while being a known predator is immediately punctured by the Fox's dry observation, revealing that the hypocrite's only true audience for his performance is himself.

memes.label: Ingratitude
memes.description: The failure to acknowledge benefits received — Aesop's Fables condemn ingratitude as a fundamental breach of reciprocity that poisons the well of future kindness.

memes.label: Ingratitude Brings Its Own Punishment
memes.description: "A hunted Stag hides beneath a Vine, escapes the huntsmen, then turns and eats the leaves that sheltered him — drawing the hunters back and dying by their arrow, confessing he deserved his fate."
aliases: ingratitude sometimes brings its own punishment
biting the hand that feeds you

memes.label: Injuries Are Never Forgotten
memes.description: "A Labourer's son is killed by a Snake. The father wounds the Snake but fails to kill it. When he later pretends reconciliation, the Snake replies: \"I can never be your friend because of my lost tail, nor you mine because of your lost child.\" Mutual injury creates an irreparable barrier."
aliases: wounds do not heal in presence of injurer

memes.label: Insult
memes.description: "A deliberate act of disrespect or provocation — in Aesop's Fables, insult is the spark that ignites conflict, the word or deed that transforms latent hostility into open enmity, and the trigger that sets revenge in motion."

memes.label: The Jackdaw
memes.description: A small corvid that appears in four of Aesop's Fables as the embodiment of vain ambition, foolish imitation, and the danger of pretending to be what one is not.

memes.label: Jealousy
memes.description: In Aesop's Fables, jealousy is a destructive force that breeds self-destructive imitation, fractures alliances, and invites external predators — the jealous rarely gain, and often lose everything.

memes.label: Juno
memes.description: Queen of the gods in Aesop's Fables — the divine consort of Jupiter, arbiter of complaints about natural endowments, and wielder of the power to transform, whose judgments reveal the folly of envying others' gifts.

memes.label: Jupiter
memes.description: King of the gods in Aesop's Fables — supreme arbiter who assigns attributes, grants kings, and judges complaints, yet his decrees often expose the folly of those who petition him.

memes.label: Kindness
memes.description: In Aesop's Fables, kindness is a strategic investment — an act of generosity from the strong to the weak that may yield an unexpected return, but only when extended without calculation and to worthy recipients.

memes.label: Kindness Wasted on the Evil
memes.description: "A Farmer revives a frozen Viper with the warmth of his own body, and the Viper's first act upon recovery is to bite its benefactor fatally — the dying man confessing he deserved his fate for pitying a villain."
aliases: kindness is thrown away upon the evil

memes.label: The Lamb
memes.description: The youngest and most defenseless of Aesop's prey animals — the Lamb embodies pure innocence, a creature whose only role is to be devoured, and whose reasoned defenses are powerless against predatory appetite.

memes.label: Learn from the Misfortunes of Others
memes.description: After a Lion, a Fox, and an Ass hunt together, the Ass divides the spoils into three equal parts — and the Lion tears him to pieces. The Fox, asked to divide next, gives almost everything to the Lion. "How did you learn?" asks the Lion. "I took a lesson from the Ass," replies the Fox.

memes.label: You Cannot Believe a Liar Even When He Tells the Truth
memes.description: The Aesopic warning that a reputation for dishonesty destroys one's credibility irreversibly — the Shepherd's Boy who cried "Wolf!" for amusement finds that when a real wolf comes, no one responds.

memes.label: Look Before Your Leap
memes.description: The Aesopic injunction against impulsive action — a Goat, driven by thirst, jumps into a well without asking how to get out, and a Fox who got there first uses the Goat's body as a ladder to escape, leaving him trapped.

memes.label: Look Which Way the Wind Blows
memes.description: The Aesopic counsel of situational awareness — a Bat, caught twice by two different Weasels, escapes first by claiming to be a mouse and then by claiming to be a bird, because it knows which identity each captor will spare.

memes.label: Men Do Not See Their Own Faults
memes.description: "Every man carries two bags: one in front containing his neighbours' faults and one behind containing his own. Hence men see the faults of others but never their own. The moral is a concise diagnosis of a universal cognitive bias — selective self-exemption from moral scrutiny."
aliases: do not see your own faults
see others faults not your own

memes.label: Mercury
memes.description: The messenger god of Aesop's Fables — Jupiter's agent on earth who tests human honesty, exacts divine punishment, and exposes the gap between self-image and reality.

memes.label: Mercy
memes.description: In Aesop's Fables, mercy is a rare plea from the weak to the strong — sometimes granted as strategic investment, more often denied when it conflicts with the powerful party's interest.

memes.label: Might Makes Right
memes.description: The Aesopic recognition that in a world without impartial enforcement, physical power determines outcomes regardless of justice — from the Lion who claims all three shares of the hunt to the Wolf who devours the Lamb after a sham trial.

memes.label: Minerva
memes.description: The goddess of wisdom in Aesop's Fables — appearing as divine counselor who advises restraint, her brief but decisive interventions embody the fable tradition's appeal to reason over force.

memes.label: Misfortune
memes.description: "An adverse event or stroke of bad luck — in Aesop's Fables, misfortune is a test: it reveals the sincerity of friendships, exposes the limits of one's abilities, and sometimes arrives from the most unexpected quarter."

memes.label: Misfortune From an Unexpected Quarter
memes.description: "A one-eyed Stag watches the land for hunters, turning his blind side to the sea — and is shot by sailors from the very direction he dismissed as harmless."
aliases: misfortune often assails us from an unexpected quarter

memes.label: Misfortune Tests Friendship
memes.description: "When a Bear menaces two Travellers, one scrambles up a tree leaving his companion to feign death on the ground. The Bear whispers a lasting truth to the abandoned man: misfortune reveals who your real friends are."

memes.label: More Consequence in Our Own Eyes
memes.description: The Aesopic observation that individuals systematically overestimate their own importance to others — a Gnat perches on a Bull's horn for hours, then ceremoniously apologizes for leaving, only to learn the Bull never noticed its arrival.

memes.label: Much Wants More and Loses All
memes.description: The Aesopic warning that insatiable appetite destroys the very source of prosperity — a Man and his Wife, dissatisfied with one golden egg per day, kill the Goose to extract all the gold at once, and find nothing but ordinary goose innards.
aliases: the goose that laid the golden eggs

memes.label: Necessity Is the Mother of Invention
memes.description: The Aesopic maxim, drawn from The Crow and the Pitcher, that urgent need — the crow's thirst within sight of unreachable water — triggers creative problem-solving unavailable to the comfortable and the well-supplied.

memes.label: No Friend Plays Double
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that friendship and duplicity cannot coexist — a Hound that alternately bites and plays with a captured Hare is denounced: "If you are my friend, why do you bite me? If you are my enemy, why do you play with me?"

memes.label: No Use Being Your Own Master
memes.description: The Aesopic warning that freedom is worthless without the strength to defend it: the Wild Ass boasts of his liberty over the Pack-Ass's servitude, but when a Lion appears, the Pack-Ass's master provides protection the Wild Ass cannot summon for himself.

memes.label: It's No Use Trying to Hide What Can't Be Hidden
memes.description: A Goatherd breaks a goat's horn with a stone and, in panic, begs the goat not to tell his master. The goat replies: "You silly fellow, my horn would cry aloud even if I held my tongue." The broken horn itself is the testimony — no silence can conceal a visible fact.

memes.label: There Is No Virtue in Giving What Is Useless
memes.description: The Aesopic observation that offering others what you yourself cannot use is not generosity but theater — a Wolf, unable to eat oats, offers them magnanimously to a Horse, who replies that if wolves could eat oats the Wolf would hardly have thought of the Horse's enjoyment.

memes.label: Notoriety Is Often Mistaken for Fame
memes.description: The Aesopic observation that public visibility is not the same as honor — a biting Dog wears his warning bell as a medal until an older dog explains it is a badge of disgrace.

memes.label: Once Bitten, Twice Shy
memes.description: "A Dog, having narrowly escaped being eaten by a Wolf, refuses to be lured out of safety a second time — experience of danger teaches caution that no promise can override."

memes.label: One Good Turn Deserves Another
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that kindness should be repaid with kindness — an Eagle, freed from a Serpent's coils by a Countryman, later saves the Countryman's life by knocking a poisoned drinking-horn from his hand, proving that gratitude operates across species and that good deeds create moral debts that must be honored.

memes.label: One Swallow Does Not Make Summer
memes.description: The Aesopic warning against drawing sweeping conclusions from a single data point — a Spendthrift sees one Swallow, sells his coat for summer, and freezes when winter returns, because one swallow does not make summer.

memes.label: Out of the Frying-Pan into the Fire
memes.description: "A Stag, fleeing from hounds, takes refuge in a cave — only to find a Lion waiting: escaping one danger delivers him straight into a worse one."
aliases: out of the frying pan into the fire
escaping one danger only to meet a greater

memes.label: Past Sufferings Revive Painful Memories
memes.description: "A Swallow urges a Nightingale to leave the woods and live among humans. The Nightingale refuses: she once lived among men and suffered cruel wrongs there. The mere thought of returning — seeing the same rooftops — revives the pain of those old wounds."

memes.label: Peace
memes.description: A desirable but precarious state in Aesop's Fables — genuine peace requires either the absence of predators or the wisdom to recognize when a peace offer is a trap.

memes.label: Persuasion
memes.description: The act of moving another to belief or action through speech — Aesop's Fables distinguish honest persuasion from self-interested manipulation, and warn that the persuader's identity matters as much as the argument.

memes.label: Persuasion Is Better Than Force
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that gentle persuasion achieves what brute force cannot — demonstrated by the Sun's triumph over the North Wind and echoed across multiple fables.

memes.label: Physician, Heal Thyself
memes.description: The Aesopic challenge to those who profess to cure others while neglecting their own defects — a Frog proclaims himself a physician capable of curing all diseases, but a Fox exposes the contradiction: the Frog cannot even cure his own lame legs and blotched skin.

memes.label: Pity
memes.description: The feeling of sorrow aroused by the suffering or misfortune of another — a complex moral force in Aesop's Fables that can be an expression of virtue, a dangerous misallocation of sympathy, or a tool of manipulation.

memes.label: Play a Good Card Once Too Often
memes.description: The Aesopic warning that a successful trick, repeated once too often, rebounds against its user — an Ass deliberately falls in a stream to lighten his salt load, but when his master substitutes sponges, the same trick becomes catastrophic.

memes.label: Power
memes.description: The capacity to act or exert control — Aesop's Fables portray power as distributed unevenly by nature, easily lost through folly, and most stable when exercised with restraint rather than force.

memes.label: Precautions Are Useless After the Event
memes.description: A caged Singing-bird, caught because she sang in the daytime, now sings only at night. A Bat tells her that this precaution is worthless now that she is already imprisoned — if she had only done so before, she might still be free. The moral exposes the futility of adopting safeguards only after the damage is already done.
aliases: closing the stable door after the horse has bolted

memes.label: Prevarication Often Amounts to Perjury
memes.description: "Two Men steal meat from a Butcher's stall: one takes it, the other hides it. The one who took it says he hasn't got it; the one who has got it says he hasn't taken it — both evade the truth, and both are guilty."

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: An inflated sense of one's own worth or importance — Aesop's Fables treat pride as the prelude to humiliation, with the proud invariably brought low by the very circumstances they presumed to transcend.

memes.label: Pride Comes Before a Fall
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that excessive self-regard invites disaster — stated explicitly in the Chesterton preface and demonstrated across dozens of fables in which the proud are brought low.

memes.label: Promise
memes.description: The act of binding oneself to a future action by word alone — Aesop's Fables test promises relentlessly, exposing the gap between what is sworn and what is delivered.

memes.label: You May Punish a Thief, but His Bent Remains
memes.description: The Aesopic observation that punishment can change form but not fundamental nature — Jupiter transforms thieving men into Ants, but the Ants continue to steal grain exactly as they did when human.

memes.label: Punishment
memes.description: In Aesop's Fables, punishment is portrayed as inevitable — whether delivered by the gods, by nature, or by the self-destructive logic of vice itself. Wrongdoing contains its own penalty.

memes.label: Quality, Not Quantity
memes.description: The Aesopic assertion that a single thing of genuine worth outweighs a multitude of lesser things — a Lioness, mocked by a Vixen for having only one cub, replies that her one is a lion.
aliases: better one lion than many foxes

memes.label: Quarrel
memes.description: "An open conflict between parties — in Aesop's Fables, quarrels are always costly, frequently pointless, and invariably exploited by third parties who wait in the wings for the combatants to exhaust themselves."

memes.label: Recover What Is Lost Over Acquiring What Is Lacked
memes.description: "A Bat, a Bramble, and a Seagull go into partnership on a trading voyage. All is lost in a storm. Ever since, each partner compulsively searches for what they lost: the Seagull scans the sea for his lead, the Bat hides from creditors by day, and the Bramble catches at every passerby's clothes. The moral diagnoses a universal human bias: the pain of loss outweighs the desire for gain."
aliases: more concerned to recover what they lose
what is lost matters more than what is lacked

memes.label: Respect
memes.description: The acknowledgment of worth or authority in another, portrayed in Aesop's Fables as something that must be earned through wisdom or genuine merit — never claimed, and dangerous to mistake for oneself.

memes.label: Revenge
memes.description: The impulse to retaliate for a perceived wrong — Aesop's Fables consistently warn that revenge is a double-edged sword that harms the avenger as often as the target.

memes.label: Revenge Is a Two-Edged Sword
memes.description: The Aesopic warning that revenge wounds both target and avenger — a Farmer, enraged by a Fox that steals his fowls, sets the Fox's tail on fire, only to watch the blazing Fox run through his ripening corn and burn his entire harvest.

memes.label: Reward
memes.description: What one receives in return for an action — Aesop's Fables are suspicious of reward, showing repeatedly that what looks like a prize may be a mark of disgrace, and that those who expect reward are often disappointed.

memes.label: Ridicule
memes.description: The social weapon of collective mockery — in Aesop's Fables, ridicule punishes pretension and unearned ambition, serving as the community's most accessible instrument for enforcing status boundaries.

memes.label: Ruin
memes.description: The state of total loss or destruction — in Aesop's Fables almost always self-inflicted, arriving not from external catastrophe but from internal folly, greed, or the failure to recognize one's own limitations.

memes.label: Sacrifice
memes.description: In Aesop's Fables, sacrifice appears both as religious ritual and as the unintended cost of folly — animals led to altars, vows made in desperation, and the ironic discovery that what is offered is often what one cannot afford to lose.

memes.label: Safety
memes.description: The condition of being protected from danger or harm — in Aesop's Fables a precarious achievement that requires constant vigilance, often demands sacrifice of freedom, and is never permanently guaranteed.

memes.label: Self-Help Is the Best Help
memes.description: The Aesopic assertion that relying on one's own efforts is more reliable than waiting for others — a Mother Lark, hearing the Farmer threaten to ask friends and then hire men to reap the field, only moves her brood when he resolves to do the work himself.

memes.label: Servants Don't Know a Good Master Till They've Served a Worse
memes.description: "An Ass, discontent with his Gardener master, is sold to a Potter, then to a Tanner. Each new master is harsher than the last. Only when facing the tanning-vat does he realise the Gardener was the best of the three — and now it is too late."
aliases: servants know good master after worse
you don't know what you have till you lose it

memes.label: Service Met with Ingratitude
memes.description: "Two Travellers shelter under a Plane-tree from the scorching sun, then call it useless because it bears no fruit — provoking the tree's indignant rebuke that they abuse their benefactor even while enjoying its shade."
aliases: many a service is met with ingratitude

memes.label: Shame
memes.description: The painful consciousness of having fallen short before others — Aesop's Fables depict shame as a social weapon deployed to control behavior, but one that can be neutralized by those who refuse to feel it.

memes.label: The Sheep
memes.description: The quintessential victim of Aesop's Fables — gentle, defenseless, and innocent, the sheep embodies the cost of vulnerability in a world ruled by predators.

memes.label: Show Gratitude Where Gratitude Is Due
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that gratitude must be directed to its true source — a Farmer thanks the Goddess of Earth for found gold, only to be corrected by Fortune, who actually bestowed the gift.

memes.label: Slow and Steady Wins the Race
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that consistent effort defeats sporadic brilliance — stated verbatim at the close of The Hare and the Tortoise and echoed across dozens of fables valuing persistence over speed.

memes.label: Strength
memes.description: Physical or collective power, treated in Aesop's Fables as a quality that must be paired with wisdom or unity to be effective — raw strength alone is frequently outmaneuvered or broken.

memes.label: The Bull
memes.description: One of Aesop's strongest creatures, appearing across seven fables as both gullible victim and shrewd survivor — a test case for the limits of physical strength against cunning and flattery.

memes.label: The Cat
memes.description: The domestic predator of Aesop's Fables — cunning, hypocritical, and proof that a change of form cannot change essential nature, the Cat appears as trickster, false friend, and reluctant goddess-made bride.

memes.label: The Covetous Are Poor Givers
memes.description: The Aesopic observation that those who habitually take from others are incapable of generosity — a hungry Viper begs every tool in a carpenter's shop for food, and the File replies that it takes from everyone and gives nothing back.

memes.label: The Doctor
memes.description: The physician figure of Aesop's Fables — nearly always a fraud, the Doctor appears as a quack who cannot heal himself, a cobbler turned healer, and a target of the sharpest satire on professional pretension.

memes.label: The Dolphin
memes.description: Intelligent sea creature of Aesop's Fables, appearing as rescuer, proud combatant, and relentless predator across three fables — a creature of decisive action whose judgment can be corrupted by pride.

memes.label: The Eagle
memes.description: King of birds in Aesop's Fables, endowed with strength by Fate itself — appears as predator, betrayer, victim, and grateful ally across ten fables.

memes.label: The Farmer
memes.description: The agricultural worker archetype in Aesop's Fables, representing rural life, practical labor, and the grounded perspective that contrasts with aristocratic pretension and animal fable fantasy.

memes.label: The Fox
memes.description: The cunning trickster of Aesop's Fables — clever, observant, and eloquent, yet often undone by his own schemes when appetite overrides prudence.

memes.label: The Fox and the Grapes
memes.description: The first fable in the Jones translation — a hungry Fox, unable to reach a bunch of grapes, dismisses them as sour, giving the world the concept of cognitive dissonance avant la lettre.

memes.label: The Goat
memes.description: The goat of Aesop's Fables — stubborn yet sacrificial, appearing as livestock, wild prey, and symbol of indiscriminate destruction when desperation rules.

memes.label: The Hare
memes.description: The swift but vulnerable prey of Aesop's Fables — appearing as quarry of eagle and hunter, symbol of the weak seeking protection, and every creature that must run to survive.

memes.label: The Hare and the Tortoise
memes.description: Aesop's most famous fable about persistence versus speed — a Hare, confident in his natural swiftness, naps mid-race while a Tortoise plods steadily to victory.

memes.label: The Horse
memes.description: The noble steed of Aesop's Fables — appearing as war-horse, mount, beast of burden, and symbol of the tension between past glory and present servitude.

memes.label: The Hunter
memes.description: The huntsman archetype in Aesop's Fables who sets traps to capture wild animals — but embodies the theme of role reversal, where the predator may become prey and the hunter may become the hunted.

memes.label: The Lion
memes.description: The apex predator of Aesop's Fables, embodying both royal authority and the limits of brute power — alternately noble, gullible, grateful, and dangerous.

memes.label: The Master
memes.description: The Master is a recurring authority figure in Aesop's Fables, representing power, ownership, and the moral complexities of hierarchical relationships between rulers and the ruled.

memes.label: The Monkey
memes.description: A primate character in Aesop's Fables that embodies the themes of blind parental love, pretentiousness, and the folly of claiming knowledge one does not possess.

memes.label: The Mouse
memes.description: A small rodent in Aesop's Fables that embodies the theme of the underdog — physically weak but capable of repaying kindness, outwitting predators, and proving that size is no measure of worth.

memes.label: The Net Is Spread in Vain in Sight of the Bird
memes.description: "A Lion invites a Bull to dinner, claiming he is sacrificing a sheep. The Bull arrives, sees an array of saucepans and spits but no sheep, and quietly walks away. The moral observes that visible preparations for harm defeat their own purpose — a trap once seen is a trap rendered useless."

relations.label: better servitude with safety than freedom with danger

memes.label: The Servant
memes.description: A domestic archetype in Aesop's Fables representing the dynamics of servitude, loyalty, class tension, and the unintended consequences of resentment against one's station.

memes.label: The Shepherd
memes.description: The Shepherd is a recurring human archetype in Aesop's Fables, representing guardianship, vigilance, and the moral responsibility of those entrusted with the care of others.

memes.label: The Snake
memes.description: The most consistently villainous creature in Aesop's Fables — a predator whose nature cannot be changed by kindness, whose memory of injury is absolute, and whose venom defines the limits of trust, reconciliation, and pity.

memes.label: The Stag
memes.description: The Stag is a forest prey animal in Aesop's Fables, symbolizing the tension between pride in appearance and vulnerability to predators, and the dangers of misplaced values.

memes.label: The Swallow
memes.description: Bird of spring and summer in Aesop's Fables, appearing as seasonal sign, boaster of noble lineage, domestic advocate, and possessor of transient beauty — a creature defined by temporariness and premature confidence.

memes.label: The Thief
memes.description: The Thief is a recurring human archetype in Aesop's Fables, embodying dishonesty, stealth, and the moral consequences of taking what belongs to others.

memes.label: There Are Two Sides to Every Question
memes.description: "A Man and a Lion argue about who is stronger, each claiming superiority. They come upon a statue of a Man strangling a Lion. The Man sees proof; the Lion replies: 'That is only your view of the case. If we Lions could make statues, you would see the Man underneath.' The moral teaches that representation is not neutral — whoever controls the telling controls the apparent truth."
aliases: the Man and the Lion statue

memes.label: Think Twice Before You Act
memes.description: Two Frogs abandon their dried-up marsh and discover a deep well. One is ready to jump in at once, tempted by the cool dampness. The wiser frog hesitates: "Supposing this well dried up like the marsh, how should we get out again?" The moral distils a principle of irreversible decision-making — do not commit to a course from which there is no retreat without first evaluating the exit conditions.

memes.label: Those Who Pretend Are Exposed
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that false pretension is self-defeating — a Crow who tries to pass as a prophetic Raven is immediately dismissed as "only a crow," and all pretenders are unmasked by the gap between performance and reality.
aliases: you cannot pretend to be what you are not

memes.label: Those Who Will Not Work Deserve to Starve
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that idleness forfeits any claim to provision — a Blacksmith's Dog sleeps through the labor but wakes for the meal, and the master's rebuke defines a moral that runs through fables of the lazy and the industrious.

memes.label: Traitors Deserve Their Fate
memes.description: "The Wolves persuade the Dogs to betray their human masters and hand over the flocks — then tear the Dogs to pieces the moment they enter the Wolves' den. The moral is blunt: those who betray trust for promises of reward will be destroyed by the very parties they collude with."

memes.label: The Traveller
memes.description: A recurring human archetype in Aesop's Fables — the wayfarer whose encounters on the road test character, reveal hidden truths, and expose the vagaries of fortune.

memes.label: Treachery
memes.description: The violation of trust or faith between parties who share a bond of friendship, protection, or hospitality — depicted in Aesop's Fables as an act that inevitably brings retribution, often through the very mechanism of the betrayal.

memes.label: Trickery
memes.description: The use of cunning, deception, and clever tricks to achieve one's ends — a recurring theme in Aesop's Fables where tricksters often succeed but sometimes face consequences.

memes.label: Trust
memes.description: The willingness to place one's welfare in another's hands — Aesop's Fables treat trust as a high-stakes wager that is easily lost and nearly impossible to regain once betrayed.

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: Correspondence between word and fact — Aesop's Fables treat truth as both a moral good and a practical necessity, because liars destroy their own credibility and cannot be believed even when they speak truly.

memes.label: Two Blacks Do Not Make a White
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that one bad act does not justify another — a Sheep refuses to trust a Stag backed by a Wolf as surety, observing that two untrustworthy parties compound rather than cancel each other's unreliability.
aliases: two wrongs do not make a right

memes.label: Union Is Strength
memes.description: "A father, frustrated by his sons' constant quarrelling, asks each to break a bundle of sticks. All fail. Then he separates the sticks — and each snaps easily. The lesson: united, you are strong; divided, you are at your enemies' mercy."
aliases: united we stand divided we fall

memes.label: Utility Is Most Men's Test of Worth
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that practical utility, not beauty or sentiment, is the standard by which most people judge value — a Peasant nearly cuts down a barren apple-tree that shelters sparrows, only to discover it is hollow and full of honey.

memes.label: A Villain May Disguise Himself
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that disguise, however elaborate, cannot fool the observant — a Cat dressed as a doctor is denied entry by ailing Birds who recognize the predator beneath the costume.

memes.label: The Want of a Good Excuse
memes.description: The Aesopic principle that the absence of a valid justification has never deterred wrongdoing — a Cat catches a Cock, fabricates a flimsy pretext about crowing disturbing sleep, and when the Cock refutes it, eats him anyway.

memes.label: The Weak May Avenge Upon the Strong
memes.description: "An Eagle devours a Hare under the protection of a tiny Beetle, dismissing the insect as beneath notice. The Beetle, patient and methodical, destroys every egg the Eagle lays — and when Jupiter himself shields the eggs, the Beetle tricks the god into breaking them."
aliases: the weak will sometimes find ways to avenge an insult even upon the strong

memes.label: The Weasel
memes.description: A small carnivorous mammal appearing in three of Aesop's Fables as a fierce predator of mice and birds, whose rigid principles and predatory nature are consistently outwitted.

memes.label: What Is Worth Most Is Often Valued Least
memes.description: The Aesopic warning that we systematically undervalue what serves us best while prizing what merely looks impressive — a Stag scorns the legs that could save him and glorifies the antlers that kill him.

memes.label: Wisdom
memes.description: In Aesop's Fables, wisdom is the fruit of age and experience — the ability to see through deception, recognize danger, and counsel restraint where the young and foolish rush toward ruin.

memes.label: The Wolf
memes.description: The principal villain of Aesop's Fables — a remorseless predator whose strength serves cruelty, and whose every appearance teaches a lesson about power without conscience.
